LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS GREGORY YOCH, JAMES DEVINCENTI AND MARCUS & MILLICHAP REAL ESTATE INVESTMENT SERVICES, INC.'S MOTION FOR PREFILING ORDER FOR VEXATIOUS LITIGANT AND TO REQUIRE SECURITY IS GRANTED. DEFENDANTS HAVE SHOWN THAT AT LEAST 5 CASES WERE DETERMINED AGAINST ENG IN THE PAST 7 YEARS. DEFENDANTS ALSO SHOW THAT PLAINTIFF'S CASE LACKS MERIT, AND PLAINTIFF DOES NOT ADDRESS THE MERITS OF THE INSTANT ACTION. PLAINTIFF MUST POST A BOND IN THE AMOUNT OF $100,000 WITHIN 15 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. THE ACTION IS STAYED PENDING PROOF THAT SUCH SECURITY HAS BEEN FURNISHED. IF THE BOND IS NOT TIMELY POSTED, DEFENDANTS MAY SEEK DISMISSAL ON THE EX PARTE CALENDAR. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (PLAINTIFF PRESENTED THE COURT WITH AN UNENDORSED DOCUMENT RE CCP 170.6 CHALLENGE THAT THE COURT FOUND AS UNTIMELY.) (PLAINTIFF FILED A DISMISSAL WITH THE CLERK'S OFFICE THIS MORNING PRIOR TO THIS HEARING. THE COURT BELIEVED THAT THAT FILING WOULD NOT AFFECT THIS RULING.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF MARTIN ENG'S MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION OF ORDER DISMISSING ENTIRE ACTION WITH PREJUDICE (CCP SECTION 1008). RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO FOLLOW PROPER PROCEDURE IN REQUESTING THIS RELIEF. FURTHER, THE COURT HAD JURISDICTION TO DISMISS THE ACTION WITH PREJUDICE AND PLAINTIFF FAILS TO PROVIDE AN AFFADAVIT CITING NEW FACTS OR LEGAL AUTHORITY TO JUSTIFY GRANTING OF THIS MOTION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (302/LMG) JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI, REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998
MINI-MINUTES FOR APR-04-2011 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302
DECLARATION IN SUPPORT OF MOTION FILED BY PLAINTIFF ENG, MARTIN

LAW AND MOTION, 302, PLAINTIFF MARTIN ENG'S MOTION TO VACATE THE PREFILING ORDER AND REMOVE MARTIN ENG FROM VEXATIOUS LITIGANT LIST PER CCP SEC 391.8, IS TAKEN OFF CALENDAR. UNDER CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 391.8, AN APPLICATION TO VACATE A VEXATIOUS LITIGANT PREFILING ORDER "SHALL BE MADE BEFORE THE JUSTICE OR JUDGE WHO ENTERED THE ORDER, IF THAT JUSTICE OR JUDGE IS AVAILABLE," AND IF NOT, "BEFORE THE PRESIDING JUSTICE OR PRESIDING JUDGE, OR HIS OR HER DESIGNEE." ACCORDINGLY, THE APPLICATION MUST BE MADE TO PRESIDING JUDGE WONG IN DEPT. 206. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, NOT REPORTED; DEPT. 302.

Off Calendar Dec-19-2019 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF MARTIN ENG Martin Eng's Motion To Vacate The Prefiling Order And Remove Martin Eng From Vexatious Litigant List Per Ccp Sec 391.8.

Off calendar. Under Code of Civil Procedure section 391.8, an application to vacate a vexatious litigant prefiling order "shall be made before the justice or judge who entered the order, if that justice or judge is available," and if not, "before the presiding justice or presiding judge, or his or her designee." Here, Judge Charlotte Walter Woolard, who entered the prefiling order on February 2, 2011, has since retired from the bench, and accordingly is not available to hear the application. Accordingly, the application must be made to Presiding Judge Wong in Dept. 206.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)

Notice And Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Dismissing Entire Action With Prejudice
Off Calendar Apr-04-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
PLAINTIFF MARTIN ENG'S MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION OF ORDER DISMISSING ENTIRE ACTION WITH PREJUDICE (CCP SECTION 1008).

APPEARANCES: Jill B. Rowe, Esq. of Cooper White & Cooper 433-1900 for defts & opposing parties; no other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling - Motion for reconsideration denied. Plaintiff has failed to follow proper procedure in requesting this relief. Further, the court had jurisdiction to dismiss the action with prejudice and plaintiff fails to provide an affadavit citing new facts or legal authority to justify granting of this motion. Order signed in open court. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Prefiling Order For Vexatious Litigant And To Require Security; Request For Judicial Notice
Off Calendar Nov-09-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANTS GREGORY YOCH, JAMES DEVINCENTI AND MARCUS & MILLICHAP REAL ESTATE INVESTMENT SERVICES, INC.'S MOTION FOR PREFILING ORDER FOR VEXATIOUS LITIGANT AND TO REQUIRE SECURITY.

APPEARANCES: Martin Eng, 246-1111 pltf & opposing party in propria persona; Jill B. Rowe, Esq. of Cooper, White & Cooper LLP 433-1900 for defts & moving parties.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - GRANTED. DEFENDANTS HAVE SHOWN THAT AT LEAST 5 CASES WERE DETERMINED AGAINST ENG IN THE PAST 7 YEARS. DEFENDANTS ALSO SHOW THAT PLAINTIFF'S CASE LACKS MERIT, AND PLAINTIFF DOES NOT ADDRESS THE MERITS OF THE INSTANT ACTION. PLAINTIFF MUST POST A BOND IN THE AMOUNT OF $100,000 WITHIN 15 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. THE ACTION IS STAYED PENDING PROOF THAT SUCH SECURITY HAS BEEN FURNISHED. IF THE BOND IS NOT TIMELY POSTED, DEFENDANTS MAY SEEK DISMISSAL ON THE EX PARTE CALENDAR. Order signed in open court. (Plaintiff presented the Court with an unendorsed document re CCP 170.6 challenge that the Court found as untimely.) (Plaintiff filed a dismissal with the clerk's office this morning prior to this hearing. The Court believed that that filing would not affect this ruling.) (302/CWW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797
